text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy town called Hawthorne, there lived a wise old owl named Ottwa. Ottwa hadn’t seen much of the world outside his tree home. One foggy morning, he decided to leave. That’s when he met Luna.

Luna was a mysterious fox. She didn’t talk much, but she seemed interested in Ottwa. They wandered around together. Luna showed Ottwa flowers from somewhere strange. They climbed up ancient hills. Something inside Ottwa pushed him higher.

They reached a lake. There was an old stone bridge. Ottwa sat on a rock. Luna sat beside him. They chatted.

Afternoon sun warmed their bones. Ottwa wondered about Luna.

“Why do you wander?”

“There’s something missing.”

“What?”

“They call it dreams.”

“Oh?” Ottwa tilted his head.

“I’ve had some.”

Ottwa was quiet for a while.

“You know I’ve watched.”

“Yes.”

“Do sky?”

“You?”
